findings.message: The `audit-prepare` command docstring states 'Output is JSON on stdout; consumed by /reviewers:audit.' but with the new `--split-to` flag the command now also writes an `index.json` + `items/item-NNNN.json` tree to disk. The docstring is slightly stale — consider a one-line note describing the side-effect when `--split-to` is set.
findings.suggestion: Append: 'When --split-to DIR is set, also writes a split-manifest layout (index.json + items/item-NNNN.json) under DIR for the Workflow tool.'

findings.message: `_emit_audit_prep` gained a new `split_to` parameter but has no function-level docstring. An inline comment block explains the split-manifest write, which is helpful, but a short docstring on the helper would make its dual-output contract (stdout manifest + optional on-disk split layout) explicit to readers.
findings.suggestion: Add a docstring summarizing: emits the audit-mode manifest to stdout; when split_to is non-empty, also writes a small index.json + items/item-NNNN.json split layout (perm 0700/0600) under split_to.
